channel.title: Drupal Core
channel.description: Stay informed about developments in Drupal Core.

item.title: #3590050: Deprecate and replace locale_status related functions
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Core
  • Type: Normal task
  • Version: 11.4.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 18 files, +453/−313 · php +453/−313
  • Diff: f6c6aec
  • Discussion: #3590050 · 4 contributors · 37 comments
  • Followers: 5

Summary

Five procedural functions in the locale module that managed translation status have been deprecated in Drupal 11.4.0 and will be removed in 13.0.0. locale_translation_get_status, locale_translation_status_save, locale_translation_status_delete_languages, locale_translation_status_delete_projects, and locale_translation_clear_status are replaced by equivalent methods on the LocaleSource service. Custom modules or install profiles that call any of these functions must update before upgrading to Drupal 13.

Impact

Affects module maintainers.

  • Deprecation: Five locale procedural functions deprecated in 11.4.0 and removed in 13.0.0: locale_translation_get_status, locale_translation_status_save, locale_translation_status_delete_languages, locale_translation_status_delete_projects, and locale_translation_clear_status replaced by methods on the LocaleSource service.
  • New API: New LocaleTranslationSource value object class and new methods loadSources(), loadSource(), saveSource(), deleteSources(), deleteSourcesByLanguage(), clearSources(), updateLastChecked(), and getLastChecked() added to the LocaleSource service.

Technical details

The LocaleSource service gained new public methods: loadSources(), loadSource(), saveSource(), deleteSources(), deleteSourcesByLanguage(), clearSources(), updateLastChecked(), and getLastChecked(). The key efficiency gain is that loadSources() now calls getMultiple() on the locale.translation_status key-value store for only the requested projects, rather than the unconditional loadAll() that the old locale_translation_get_status() always performed. Batch operations in LocaleFetch and LocaleProjectChecker were updated to call loadSource() per project/langcode, so each batch item touches only its own data.

A new typed value object LocaleTranslationSource (in Drupal\locale) replaces the anonymous stdClass that sourceBuild() previously returned. It carries the same public properties (project, langcode, type, timestamp, hash, last_checked, files, version, server_pattern, core, name) and is instantiated via LocaleTranslationSource::fromProject(). The last_checked timestamp previously stored in \Drupal::state() under locale.translation_last_checked is now stored inside the locale.translation_status key-value collection under the key last-checked, accessed only through LocaleSource::getLastChecked() and updateLastChecked().

The installer was further simplified in the second commit: _install_prepare_import() was removed entirely, and install_import_translations() now delegates directly to \Drupal::service(LocaleFetch::class)->buildUpdateBatch(). Installer test fixtures that previously hardcoded drupal-8.0.0.* translation filenames now use \Drupal::VERSION so the filename matches the running codebase, removing an entire category of version-matching logic that _install_prepare_import() had handled as a special case. locale_translation_build_sources() deprecation text was also corrected to point to sourceBuild() rather than the removed buildSources() method.

Upgrade

Replace the deprecated procedural calls with the LocaleSource service:

use Drupal\locale\LocaleSource;

$localeSource = \Drupal::service(LocaleSource::class);

// locale_translation_get_status($projects, $langcodes)
$sources = $localeSource->loadSources($projects, $langcodes);

// locale_translation_status_save($project, $langcode, $type, $data)
$localeSource->saveSource($project, $langcode, $type, $data);

// locale_translation_status_delete_languages($langcodes)
foreach ($langcodes as $langcode) {
  $localeSource->deleteSourcesByLanguage($langcode);
}

// locale_translation_status_delete_projects($projects)
$localeSource->deleteSources($projects);

// locale_translation_clear_status()
$localeSource->clearSources();

The return type of loadSources() is LocaleTranslationSource[][] rather than stdClass[][]. Code that previously assigned properties directly to the returned objects via $status[$project][$langcode]->type = 'current' continues to work because LocaleTranslationSource uses public typed properties, but the class must be imported.

If you previously read the last-checked timestamp directly from state (\Drupal::state()->get('locale.translation_last_checked')), use $localeSource->getLastChecked() instead. That state key is no longer written by core.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: May 14, 2026
  • First commit: June 4, 2026 (21 days later)
  • Last commit: June 9, 2026 (5 days and 4 commits later)

Key contributors

  • nicxvan (nLightened Development LLC)
  • berdir (MD Systems GmbH)
  • penyaskito (Acquia)
  • gábor hojtsy (Acquia)
  • catch (Tag1 Consulting, Inc.)

Collaboration

nicxvan and berdir collaborated closely on this issue. berdir ported analysis notes from the MR thread identifying the layered inefficiency: every call to locale_translation_get_status() loaded all project data from key-value regardless of which project was needed, and callers then called buildSources() again inside loops, producing redundant work. nicxvan uncovered the tricky installer failure caused by _install_prepare_import() relying on version-string side effects: \Drupal::service(LocaleProjectRepository::class)->set($project) was being called with the version extracted from the .po filename (e.g. 8.0.0), implicitly updating the project version so subsequent lookups matched. nicxvan diagnosed this by manually tracing the installer steps and confirmed in comment 21 that explicitly setting the file URI and filename on the loaded source and calling saveSource() was the correct replacement. berdir summarized in comment 32 that the remaining test failures on the forward-port were due to tests still constructing filenames with the old major-version pattern, and opened follow-up issues for further cleanup.

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3593281: Rename "promoted to front page" to "promoted"
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Core
  • Type: Normal task
  • Version: 11.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 41 files, +61/−61 · php +32/−32 · yml +17/−17 · twig +12/−12
  • Diff: 234a5ae
  • Discussion: #3593281 · 4 contributors · 19 comments
  • Followers: 7

Summary

The long-standing "Promoted to front page" label on nodes has been renamed to simply "Promoted" across the entire admin UI. The old name was misleading because the /node listing is not the front page on most sites and, per the related initiative (#3572350), will no longer be enabled by default. Site editors will see "Promoted" in the publishing options when creating or editing content, "Promote content" and "Unpromote content" in bulk-action menus, and updated descriptions in the Frontpage view. No database schema, field machine names, or PHP class/method names changed.

Impact

Affects site owners.

  • Config change: Labels in system.action.node_promote_action.yml, system.action.node_unpromote_action.yml, views.view.frontpage.yml, and field.field.node.*.promote.yml default config updated to use the new terminology.

Technical details

The diff touches labels and human-readable strings only; the underlying promote field, NodeInterface::PROMOTED/NOT_PROMOTED constants, and all method names (isPromoted(), etc.) are unchanged. Updated locations include: the base field definition label in Node::baseFieldDefinitions(), the NodeTypeForm publishing-options checkbox, the #[Action] annotations on PromoteNode and DemoteNode (and their corresponding system.action.node_promote_action.yml / node_unpromote_action.yml config files), Views data strings in NodeViewsData::getViewsData(), the views.view.frontpage.yml description and empty-state text, field.field.node.article.promote.yml and field.field.node.recipe.promote.yml default config, the get-started.html.twig onboarding template, node.module help text, and node.html.twig docblock comments in all core themes. Config schema labels in node.schema.yml for action.configuration.node_unpromote_action and action.configuration.node_promote_action are also updated. Because many of the renamed strings are translatable, existing interface translations (on localize.drupal.org or in custom translation files) for the old strings will no longer match and will fall back to English until updated. The full list of changed strings was documented in a change record at https://www.drupal.org/node/3593641.

Upgrade

No code changes are required. If your site has exported configuration that includes any of the affected default config files (e.g. system.action.node_promote_action.yml, system.action.node_unpromote_action.yml, views.view.frontpage.yml, or field.field.node.*.promote.yml), re-export your config after updating to pick up the new labels and avoid config-hash mismatches. If you have custom interface translations for any of the renamed strings, update them to match the new source strings listed in the change record.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: June 2, 2026
  • Committed: June 9, 2026 (6 days and 2 commits later)

Key contributors

  • gábor hojtsy (Acquia)
  • macsim
  • catch (Tag1 Consulting, Inc.)

Collaboration

Reviewer macsim noted that multiple translatable strings had been renamed and that a Change Record was needed so translation teams on localize.drupal.org would be aware of the fallback risk. gábor hojtsy then created the change record (https://www.drupal.org/node/3593641) based on the list macsim compiled in the thread.

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3453474: CLI entry point in Drupal Core
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Core
  • Type: Major feature request
  • Version: main
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 45 files, +1439/−150 · php +1394/−144 · yml +31/−1 · lock +4/−1 · json +4/−1 · dist +3 · txt +2/−2 · sh +1/−1
  • Diff: 21e3ddf
  • Discussion: #3453474 · 28 contributors · 145 comments
  • Followers: 86

Summary

Drupal core now ships a fully-featured CLI tool available as vendor/bin/dr. Out of the box it provides cache:rebuild (alias cr), system:cron (alias cron), system:status, recipe:apply, recipe:info, and content:export commands, covering the most common tasks previously requiring Drush. Module developers can add their own commands by placing a class in their module's src/Command/ directory and annotating it with #[AsCommand]; no service YAML registration is needed. The legacy core/scripts/drupal entry point still functions, and vendor/bin/dr install / vendor/bin/dr quick-start replace the old php core/scripts/drupal install invocations documented in INSTALL.md and CI scripts.

Impact

Affects module maintainers.

  • Deprecation: BootableCommandTrait::boot() deprecated in drupal:11.4.0 and removed in drupal:13.0.0; the new CLI bootstraps Drupal automatically.
  • New API: New vendor/bin/dr CLI entry point backed by DrupalApplication; modules auto-discovered via ConsoleCompilerPass scanning src/Command/ for #[AsCommand]; built-in commands cache:rebuild, system:cron, system:status, recipe:apply, recipe:info, and content:export added.

Technical details

The binary is registered in core's composer.json as scripts/dr, which makes it available as vendor/bin/dr after composer install. The script returns a Symfony Runtime application, which is now an explicit non-dev dependency (symfony/runtime ^8.0).

DrupalApplication extends Symfony's Application and provides lazy bootstrap: on first command lookup it instantiates DrupalKernel with $allow_dumping=FALSE (to avoid reusing a cached container), boots the site, pushes a request onto the stack, and then loads module-contributed commands via DrupalApplication::addDrupalCommands(). A global --url option (also readable from the DRUPAL_URI environment variable) controls which site is selected in multi-site setups. Commands that do not need a running site (e.g. InstallCommand, CacheRebuildCommand) are registered as default commands and run without bootstrapping.

ConsoleCompilerPass (a new @internal compiler pass) runs at container compile time. It iterates container.namespaces, looks inside each module's src/Command/ directory recursively, and registers any class bearing #[AsCommand] as an autowired, public service tagged console.command. It also checks the full class hierarchy to skip commands that extend optional third-party base classes (such as Drush commands) whose parents may not be installed. Symfony's own AddConsoleCommandPass then processes the tagged services into a lazy CommandLoaderInterface (console.command_loader). Commands that set their name only via configure() (without the attribute) are not auto-discovered and must be declared explicitly in a module's *.services.yml with { name: console.command }.

DrupalConsoleLogger (a PSR-3 logger) and ConsoleSubscriber (a ConsoleEvents::COMMAND listener) together route Drupal's internal log messages to the console output during command execution, with notices shown at normal verbosity and info/debug messages gated behind -v/-vvv.

Several existing @internal commands were refactored as part of this change: ContentExportCommand moved from Drupal\Core\DefaultContent to Drupal\Core\DefaultContent\Command; RecipeCommand and RecipeInfoCommand moved from Drupal\Core\Recipe to Drupal\Core\Recipe\Command. All three dropped BootableCommandTrait in favor of constructor-injected services, since DrupalApplication now handles bootstrapping automatically. QuickStartCommand was rewritten as an invokable class using #[Argument] and #[Option] attributes instead of extending Command. DrupalKernel::compileContainer() was updated to also scan components that expose a Command/ directory, ensuring core-level commands are discovered.

The full developer API is documented in a new @defgroup console_commands section in module.api.php, including both the Command-extending style and the lighter invokable-class style. Related bootstrap work on Symfony Runtime was tracked in #3313404.

Upgrade

Replace php core/scripts/drupal invocations with vendor/bin/dr:

# Before
php core/scripts/drupal install standard
php core/scripts/drupal quick-start --help

# After
vendor/bin/dr install standard
vendor/bin/dr quick-start --help

Commands that use BootableCommandTrait::boot() must be rewritten to receive their dependencies via constructor injection; DrupalApplication now boots Drupal automatically before execute() is called:

// Before
class MyCommand extends Command {
  use BootableCommandTrait;
  public function __construct($class_loader) {
    parent::__construct('my:command');
    $this->classLoader = $class_loader;
  }
  protected function execute(InputInterface $input, OutputInterface $output): int {
    $container = $this->boot()->getContainer();
    $service = $container->get(MyService::class);
    // ...
  }
}

// After — place in src/Command/MyCommand.php
#[AsCommand(name: 'my:command', description: 'My command.')]
class MyCommand extends Command {
  public function __construct(protected readonly MyService $service) {
    parent::__construct();
  }
  protected function execute(InputInterface $input, OutputInterface $output): int {
    // $this->service is already available; no manual bootstrap needed.
  }
}

Code that references the now-moved @internal classes by their old fully-qualified names must update the namespace:

| Old | New | |---|---| | Drupal\Core\DefaultContent\ContentExportCommand | Drupal\Core\DefaultContent\Command\ContentExportCommand | | Drupal\Core\Recipe\RecipeCommand | Drupal\Core\Recipe\Command\RecipeCommand | | Drupal\Core\Recipe\RecipeInfoCommand | Drupal\Core\Recipe\Command\RecipeInfoCommand |

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: June 9, 2024
  • Committed: June 8, 2026 (1 year and 3 commits later)

Key contributors

  • dpi (PreviousNext)
  • chi
  • andypost (Skilld)
  • mradcliffe (Kosada)
  • acbramley (PreviousNext)
  • aaronmchale (The University of Edinburgh)
  • bhanu951
  • greg.1.anderson (Pantheon)
  • moshe weitzman
  • gábor hojtsy (Acquia)
  • bircher (Nuvole)
  • catch (Third and Grove)
  • franksj
  • rymo
  • godotislate
  • rfay (DDEV)
  • xjm (Zoocha)
  • dww (3281d Consulting)
  • longwave (Full Fat Things)
  • alexpott (Acro Commerce)
  • arrow
  • larowlan (PreviousNext)
  • kingdutch (Open Social)

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3132725: "Limit list to selected items" on exposed filters does not filter
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Core
  • Type: Normal bug report
  • Version: 11.4.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 2 files, +43/−2 · php +43/−2
  • Diff: f7c6fbe
  • Discussion: #3132725 · 11 contributors · 51 comments
  • Followers: 13

Summary

The "Limit list to selected items" checkbox on exposed in_operator Views filters (such as Content type) has never functioned correctly. When that option was checked and the exposed filter's default "- Any -" selection was in effect, the result set was not limited to nodes matching the pre-selected filter values. The fix makes the option work as advertised: with "Limit list to selected items" enabled and "- Any -" selected, only records matching the configured filter values are returned.

Impact

No upgrade or configuration changes required.

Technical details

The root cause was a key-name mismatch in InOperator::acceptExposedInput(). The method guarded its "return TRUE when 'All' is selected and reduce is active" code path with !empty($this->options['expose']['limit']), but the expose form stores this option under the key 'reduce' (set in buildExposeForm() as $form['expose']['reduce']). Because no key named 'limit' ever exists in the expose options, the condition always evaluated to false and the block was never entered, leaving "All" input unhandled by the reduce logic. The fix is a one-character key rename from 'limit' to 'reduce' in that condition. A kernel test was added to FilterInOperatorTest::testFilterExposedReduce() that configures the age filter on the test_view with reduce => TRUE and value restricted to two ages, then asserts the result set contains only matching rows.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: April 30, 2020
  • Committed: June 8, 2026 (6 years and 3 commits later)

Key contributors

  • devad
  • dww
  • steyep
  • quietone (PreviousNext)
  • smustgrave (Mobomo)
  • carlos romero (hiberus)
  • dcam
  • godotislate

Collaboration

steyep identified the exact mismatch in a 2022 comment, pointing to the 'reduce' key in the form definition versus 'limit' in acceptExposedInput(), and provided a hook_views_pre_build() workaround. That analysis directly informed the eventual one-line fix. contributor dww, who noted he was independently affected by the bug, confirmed the MR worked and prompted the test to be converted from a unit test to a kernel test by pointing to FilterInOperatorTest as the right home. smustgrave carried out the conversion, and dww ran it locally to verify the failure-then-pass behavior before marking it RTBC.

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3594092: loadUnchanged() returns an in-memory-modified entity when hook_entity_preload() swaps in a non-default revision
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Core
  • Type: Normal bug report
  • Version: 11.4.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 2 files, +18/−0 · php +18
  • Diff: aad4502
  • Discussion: #3594092 · 3 contributors · 11 comments
  • Followers: 5

Summary

ContentEntityStorageBase::loadUnchanged() could return an entity with unsaved in-memory modifications instead of the persisted state when a module such as Workspaces swaps in a non-default revision via hook_entity_preload(). This is now fixed so the method always reflects the saved state of the entity.

Impact

No upgrade or configuration changes required.

Technical details

loadUnchanged() clears the entity-keyed static cache via parent::resetCache(), but a second static cache stores revisions keyed by {entityTypeId}:{id}:revisions. When hook_entity_preload() returns a non-default revision, the reload path goes through loadMultipleRevisions() to getFromStaticRevisionCache(), which still held the in-memory-modified object.

The fix adds a call to $this->memoryCache->invalidateTags([$revision_cache_tag]) inside loadUnchanged(), using the tag "{$this->entityTypeId}:{$id}:revisions", guarded by a check for isStaticallyCacheable() && isRevisionable(). This evicts the revision from the memory cache without touching the persistent cache, so the subsequent reload still benefits from the fast persistent-cache path rather than hitting storage unnecessarily. A new assertion in ContentEntityStorageBaseTest::testNonDefaultRevision() covers the scenario directly.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: June 7, 2026
  • Committed: June 8, 2026

Key contributors

  • amateescu
  • godotislate
  • catch (Tag1 Consulting, Inc.)

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3592577: Ensure that hook attributes are never parsed from a stale opcache
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Core
  • Type: Major bug report
  • Version: 11.4.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 2 files, +14/−0 · php +14
  • Diff: 16d92a4
  • Discussion: #3592577 · 4 contributors · 20 comments
  • Followers: 6

Summary

After deploying new code that changes OOP hook implementations (such as moving a hook to a different class), sites could end up with stale hook data in the key/value store. This happened because PHP's opcache can hold old file bytecode for up to opcache.revalidate_freq seconds (default: 2s). If a container cache rebuild started during that window, hook attribute parsing via reflection would read the old class definitions and persist them. Subsequent requests would use this stale hook data until another manual cache rebuild occurred. The fix ensures opcache is explicitly invalidated for each file before it is inspected for hook attributes during a rebuild.

Impact

No upgrade or configuration changes required.

Technical details

The hook attribute collector classes (HookCollectorPass for modules, and the equivalent for themes) walk PHP source files and parse their #[Hook] attributes using reflection. When a file has no valid entry in the file cache, the collector falls through to live reflection. The fix adds a call to OpCodeCache::invalidate($filename) at the top of that file-cache-miss branch in both collectModuleHookImplementations() and collectThemeHookImplementations(). OpCodeCache::invalidate() calls PHP's opcache_invalidate() under the hood; that function performs an mtime check and drops the cached bytecode for the file if it has changed on disk. This brings the opcache inline with the filesystem before reflection runs, preventing the reflection APIs from seeing stale class definitions. The change is confined to the cache-miss path, so it adds no overhead when the file cache is warm.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: May 28, 2026
  • Committed: June 8, 2026 (10 days and 3 commits later)

Key contributors

  • catch (Tag1 Consulting, Inc.)
  • nicxvan (nLightened Development LLC)
  • larowlan (PreviousNext)
  • alexpott (Acro Commerce)

Collaboration

larowlan reported a similar symptom on a client site (hooks going missing outside of deployments, fixed by drush cr), which prompted useful discussion about whether the same root cause was involved. After investigation with database dumps, larowlan concluded the issue on their end stemmed from bad cache contexts in custom code rather than stale opcache, narrowing the scope of this fix back to the deployment race condition described in the issue body.

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3581056: Introduce a OneTimeAuthentication service and deprecate user_pass_rehash
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Core
  • Type: Normal task
  • Version: 11.4.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 13 files, +294/−90 · php +293/−90 · yml +1
  • Diff: a38f1d1
  • Discussion: #3581056 · 10 contributors · 43 comments
  • Followers: 12

Summary

Drupal 11.4 introduces a new \Drupal\user\OneTimeAuthentication service that consolidates all one-time login and account-cancellation URL generation into one place. The four procedural functions user_pass_rehash(), user_pass_reset_url(), user_cancel_url(), and user_mail_tokens() are deprecated and will be removed in Drupal 13.0.0. Custom and contrib code that calls any of those functions will start triggering deprecation notices and must be updated before the Drupal 13 upgrade.

Impact

Affects module maintainers.

  • Deprecation: user_pass_rehash(), user_pass_reset_url(), user_cancel_url(), user_mail_tokens() deprecated in 11.4.0, removed in 13.0.0; UserController::validatePathParameters() and ServerCommand::getOneTimeLoginUrl() deprecated in 11.4.0, removed in 12.0.0
  • New API: New final service \Drupal\user\OneTimeAuthentication with generateHmac(), verifyHmac(), generateOneTimeLoginUrl(), and generateCancelConfirmUrl() methods

Technical details

The new final readonly class OneTimeAuthentication lives in core/modules/user/src/OneTimeAuthentication.php and is registered as an autowireable service (Drupal\user\OneTimeAuthentication: ~ in user.services.yml). It depends on TimeInterface and LanguageManagerInterface. Its public non-internal methods are generateHmac(), verifyHmac(), generateOneTimeLoginUrl(), and generateCancelConfirmUrl(); the tokens() method is marked @internal and serves as a drop-in callback for Token::replace().

The procedural functions in user.module now emit E_USER_DEPRECATED and delegate to the service. user_pass_reset_url() and user_cancel_url() are removed in 13.0.0; UserController::validatePathParameters() (a protected helper) is deprecated and removed in 12.0.0 because protected methods carry no BC promise. ServerCommand::getOneTimeLoginUrl() is also deprecated in 11.4.0 with no replacement and removed in 12.0.0; ServerCommand::start() now calls the service directly.

UserController gains OneTimeAuthentication as an optional constructor argument (defaulting to a service-container fallback with a deprecation notice when omitted), and its internal validatePathParameters() now delegates to verifyHmac().

A key behavioral difference: generateOneTimeLoginUrl() returns a \Drupal\Core\Url object, not a string. It accepts an $immediate bool parameter that selects the user.reset.login route (previously callers appended /login to the URL string). The old destination query-string pattern that was manually concatenated is now handled via $url->mergeOptions(['query' => ['destination' => ...]]) on the returned object.

Upgrade

Replace calls to deprecated functions with the new service methods. The service is typehintable as \Drupal\user\OneTimeAuthentication for constructor injection, or retrievable via \Drupal::service(OneTimeAuthentication::class).

// Before
$hash = user_pass_rehash($account, $timestamp);
$login_url = user_pass_reset_url($account, $options);         // returns string
$cancel_url = user_cancel_url($account, $options);            // returns string
// In token callbacks:
$token_options = ['callback' => 'user_mail_tokens', ...];
// Deprecated method on UserController:
$valid = $this->validatePathParameters($user, $timestamp, $hash, $timeout);

// After
use Drupal\user\OneTimeAuthentication;
$ota = \Drupal::service(OneTimeAuthentication::class);

$hash      = $ota->generateHmac($account, $timestamp);
$login_url = $ota->generateOneTimeLoginUrl($account, $options)->toString(); // returns Url, call toString()
$cancel_url = $ota->generateCancelConfirmUrl($account, $options)->toString();
// For immediate login (replaces appending '/login' to the URL):
$login_url = $ota->generateOneTimeLoginUrl($account, $options, immediate: TRUE)->toString();
// In token callbacks:
$token_options = ['callback' => $ota->tokens(...), ...];
// In UserController subclasses (if overriding validatePathParameters):
$valid = $this->oneTimeAuthentication->verifyHmac($user, $timestamp, $hash, $timeout);

Note: UserController::validatePathParameters() is removed in 12.0.0 (not 13.0.0), so subclasses overriding it must migrate sooner.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: March 23, 2026
  • Committed: June 8, 2026 (2 months and 3 commits later)

Key contributors

  • znerol
  • berdir (MD Systems GmbH)
  • phenaproxima (Acquia)
  • nicxvan (nLightened Development LLC)
  • benjifisher (Harvard Web Publishing)
  • heddn (MTech, LLC)
  • godotislate
  • longwave (Full Fat Things)

Collaboration

A substantive design debate ran through the thread on two points. First, berdir argued against adding a PHP interface to the service because the team does not want to support alternative implementations; this keeps it easier to add new methods without BC concerns. Second, whether to mark the class final triggered a lengthy discussion referencing a still-open policy issue (#3488476). alexpott reviewed the security-critical code paths (confirming hash_equals() was preserved) and concluded the class should ship as final to actively discourage extension; catch agreed for this specific case. nicxvan and benjifisher drove several rounds of review covering coding standards, the deprecation timeline for the protected validatePathParameters() method, and change-record completeness before the committer's sign-off.

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3588490: Don't reset the extension lists in system requirements
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Core
  • Type: Major task
  • Version: 11.4.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 3 files, +13/−7 · php +13/−7
  • Diff: 4985bc5
  • Discussion: #3588490 · 7 contributors · 25 comments
  • Followers: 9

Summary

Every admin page load was silently invalidating the cache_bootstrap cache bin. SystemRequirementsHooks::checkRequirements() called reset() on the module and theme extension lists before reading them, wiping their cached state on each request. Because system requirements are checked on every admin page, this caused constant cache rebuilds and degraded admin performance across the board. The fix adds a way to retrieve a fresh extension list without discarding the cached copy, eliminating the unnecessary invalidation.

Impact

Affects module maintainers.

  • New API: New optional bool $skip_cache = FALSE parameter on ExtensionList::getList() lets callers retrieve a fresh list without invalidating the cached copy.

Technical details

The root cause was inherited from Drupal 7's system_rebuild_module_data(), which both built and persisted extension data in one call. That pattern was ported forward and survived into SystemRequirementsHooks::checkRequirements(), which called $module_extension_list->reset() and $theme_extension_list->reset() before every call to getList().

ExtensionList::getList() now accepts an optional bool $skip_cache = FALSE parameter. When TRUE, it calls $this->doList() directly to bypass the persistent cache, but it still populates $this->extensions with the result so that subsequent calls within the same request use the fresh data without another rebuild. The old reset() approach cleared that property entirely, forcing every downstream caller in the same request to rebuild from scratch.

SystemRequirementsHooks::checkRequirements() drops its two ->reset() calls and switches to getList(TRUE). AppController::themesPage() similarly replaces $this->themeExtensionList->reset()->getList() with $this->themeExtensionList->getList(TRUE). The related issue #3336621 tracks the broader cost of running hook_requirements() on all admin pages.

Upgrade

If you call ExtensionList::getList() or chain ->reset()->getList() to force a fresh read, switch to the new parameter instead:

// Before
$extensions = $extensionList->reset()->getList();

// After
$extensions = $extensionList->getList(skip_cache: TRUE);

Using getList(TRUE) fetches fresh data while preserving the cached copy for subsequent callers in the same request, unlike reset() which drops the in-memory cache entirely.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: May 5, 2026
  • Committed: June 6, 2026 (1 month and 3 commits later)

Key contributors

  • catch (Third and Grove)
  • berdir (MD Systems GmbH)
  • alexpott (Acro Commerce)
  • nicxvan (nLightened Development LLC)
  • godotislate

Collaboration

berdir pointed out on the MR why the original code "fails so hard right now" (the reset wipes the in-memory cache for all subsequent callers in the request, not just the persistent cache), and also cross-linked #3336621 as a complementary effort to reduce how often requirements hooks run at all. alexpott noted that the new parameter needed type hints. catch traced the pattern back to Drupal 7's system_rebuild_module_data() to explain how it survived this long.

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3588276: Remove comment module dependency from standard profile and recipe
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Core
  • Type: Normal task
  • Version: 11.4.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 12 files, +9/−122 · php +9/−27 · yml +0/−95
  • Diff: 39e9528
  • Discussion: #3588276 · 4 contributors · 29 comments
  • Followers: 6

Summary

The standard install profile and its accompanying recipe no longer depend on or install the comment module. Sites installed fresh from the standard profile will not have commenting functionality enabled by default. Site builders who want comments on their content must now explicitly install the comment module and configure it themselves. This is a follow-up to #3587118, which removed the built-in Article and Basic page content types from the standard profile as part of a broader effort to slim down what the profile installs.

Impact

Affects site owners.

  • Config change: Standard profile no longer installs the comment module or any comment configuration; comment-related permissions are removed from the anonymous, authenticated, and content_editor roles.

Technical details

The commit deletes all comment-related configuration that the standard profile previously shipped: comment.type.comment, core.entity_form_display.comment.comment.default, core.entity_view_display.comment.comment.default, and field.field.comment.comment.comment_body. The comment module entry is removed from the install list in standard.info.yml.

Role configuration is also cleaned up. The user.role.anonymous role loses the comment module dependency and the access comments permission. The user.role.authenticated role loses the comment dependency along with access comments, post comments, and skip comment approval permissions. The user.role.content_editor role loses the comment dependency and edit own comments.

Test files are updated accordingly: BigPipeTest drops the post comments and skip comment approval permissions from its test user, and a stale parenthetical referring to "no comment form on test content type" is removed from a comment in that test. Performance baseline counts in NodePageCacheTest are adjusted downward to reflect the removal of the comment type config query and reduced cache lookups. The standard profile config synchronisation test in StandardTest removes expected comment-related config entries (comment.settings, comment.type.comment, views.view.comment, views.view.comments_recent, related field and display config, and comment system actions) from its changelist assertions.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: May 4, 2026
  • First commit: May 26, 2026 (22 days later)
  • Last commit: June 6, 2026 (10 days and 3 commits later)

Key contributors

  • gábor hojtsy (Acquia)
  • smustgrave (Mobomo)
  • catch (Tag1 Consulting, Inc.)

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3591076: JS translation files should be generated and served from assets://
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Core
  • Type: Normal feature request
  • Version: 11.4.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 3 files, +5/−5 · php +5/−5
  • Diff: b3b68ae
  • Discussion: #3591076 · 4 contributors · 13 comments
  • Followers: 9

Summary

The locale module now stores and serves JavaScript translation files using the assets:// stream wrapper instead of public://. Since Drupal 10.1/11.1 (#3027639), assets:// has been available as a configurable wrapper so sites can separate generated files (aggregated CSS/JS, etc.) from user-uploaded content. JS translation files were overlooked in that change and continued to use public://. On sites where assets:// is not separately configured (the default), there is no visible difference. On sites that have pointed assets:// to a separate location, locale JS files will now land there alongside other generated assets.

Impact

No upgrade or configuration changes required.

Technical details

Three places in locale.module built the translation JS directory path as 'public://' . $config->get('javascript.directory'): the locale_uninstall() cleanup routine, locale_js_translate(), and _locale_rebuild_js(). Each is now 'assets://' instead. The javascript.directory config key in locale.settings is unchanged. Corresponding assertions in LocaleJavascriptTranslationTest were updated to the new stream wrapper prefix. A change record was drafted at #3592731.

Upgrade

Sites that have configured assets:// to a different physical path than public:// will have locale JS translation files placed in the new location on the next rebuild of JS translations. Any custom code or external tooling that references those files by constructing a public:// path should be updated:

// Before
$js_file = 'public://' . $config->get('javascript.directory') . '/' . $filename;

// After
$js_file = 'assets://' . $config->get('javascript.directory') . '/' . $filename;

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: May 20, 2026
  • Committed: June 4, 2026 (15 days and 3 commits later)

Key contributors

  • guillaumepacilly

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3041170: RowPluginBase::render() update docblock and trigger deprecation for old typehint
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Core
  • Type: Normal bug report
  • Version: 11.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 1 file, +8/−4 · php +8/−4
  • Diff: 65947ba
  • Discussion: #3041170 · 9 contributors · 37 comments
  • Followers: 13

Summary

The docblock for RowPluginBase::render() in the Views system has been corrected to match what the method actually does: it returns a render array, not a string, and it expects a \Drupal\views\ResultRow object, not a generic object. A runtime deprecation notice is now triggered whenever something other than a ResultRow is passed. Sites running custom Views row plugins that pass non-ResultRow arguments will see deprecation errors on Drupal 11.4 and a hard type error on Drupal 12.

Impact

Affects module maintainers.

  • Deprecation: RowPluginBase::render() now triggers a deprecation when its $row argument is not a \Drupal\views\ResultRow; Drupal 12 will enforce this with a type error.

Technical details

The render() method in RowPluginBase has returned a render array since at least Drupal 8, but its docblock declared @return string and @param object $row. The fix corrects both annotations to @return array and @param \Drupal\views\ResultRow $row, adds the corresponding use Drupal\views\ResultRow import, and inserts an instanceof guard that calls @trigger_error(..., E_USER_DEPRECATED) when $row is not a ResultRow. The deprecation message reads: "Passing an object other than a ResultRow to RowPluginBase::render() is deprecated in drupal:11.4.0 and will throw a type error in drupal:12.0.0." The change record linked from the message is https://www.drupal.org/node/2939099. The method signature itself is unchanged; the actual ResultRow type hint is deferred to Drupal 12 to avoid an immediate hard break.

Upgrade

Any custom Views row plugin whose render() override receives, or passes through, an argument that is not a \Drupal\views\ResultRow instance must be corrected before upgrading to Drupal 12.

// Before (generic object, triggers deprecation in 11.4):
public function render($row) { ... }

// After (explicit type, compatible with Drupal 12):
public function render(\Drupal\views\ResultRow $row) { ... }

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: March 18, 2019
  • Committed: June 4, 2026 (7 years and 3 commits later)

Key contributors

  • mile23
  • mikelutz (ICON Interactive)
  • smustgrave (Mobomo)
  • lendude (iO)
  • richgerdes (Urban Emu)
  • sivaji_ganesh_jojodae
  • catch (Tag1 Consulting, Inc.)

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3593202: Drop the explicit clear of plugin caches in drupal_flush_all_caches()
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Core
  • Type: Normal task
  • Version: 11.4.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 1 file, +0/−3 · php +0/−3
  • Diff: 0394ef0
  • Discussion: #3593202 · 3 contributors · 23 comments
  • Followers: 4

Summary

Drupal's drupal_flush_all_caches() function used to call plugin.cache_clearer->clearCachedDefinitions() after wiping all cache bins and rebuilding the container. Because the container rebuild already replaces all service instances with fresh ones (discarding any in-memory plugin definition state), that explicit plugin cache clear was redundant work. The three lines have been removed, making the cache-rebuild path slightly faster with no change in correctness. The plugin.cache_clearer service itself is not removed; it is still used during module and theme install/uninstall, which does not wipe cache bins the same way.

Impact

No upgrade or configuration changes required.

Technical details

The removed call in drupal_flush_all_caches() (\Drupal::service('plugin.cache_clearer')->clearCachedDefinitions()) originally dates to 2013, when the container was not yet rebuilt on cache clear (the @todo Rebuild the kernel/container comment in issue #1892462 predates the centralized container invalidation added in #2493665). Once drupal_flush_all_caches() gained $kernel->invalidateContainer() and $kernel->rebuildContainer(), all plugin manager service instances are replaced before the plugin.cache_clearer line ever runs, so the call clears definitions that no longer exist in the active container. Manual instrumentation by longwave confirmed that by the time the removed call ran in practice, only the entity type manager retained any data, solely because BlockHooks::rebuild() (invoked earlier via hook_rebuild) had reloaded it. Two follow-up issues were opened as a result of this investigation: #3593204 to remove BlockHooks::rebuild() and #3593415 to deprecate the plugin.cache_clearer service entirely.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: June 2, 2026
  • Committed: June 3, 2026 (1 day and 3 commits later)

Key contributors

  • catch (Tag1 Consulting, Inc.)
  • longwave (Full Fat Things)
  • alexpott (Acro Commerce)

Collaboration

longwave did runtime instrumentation during the review, confirming empirically that by the time plugin.cache_clearer->clearCachedDefinitions() ran inside drupal_flush_all_caches(), only one plugin manager (the entity type manager, reloaded by BlockHooks::rebuild()) held any data. longwave also traced the full history of the call back to 2013 and established that the container rebuild introduced in #2493665 had made it redundant. That research directly moved the issue from "questionable" to "safe to drop entirely".

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3020938: Add view unpublished block content permission
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Core
  • Type: Normal feature request
  • Version: 11.4.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 5 files, +33/−6 · php +29/−6 · yml +4
  • Diff: 75becbf
  • Discussion: #3020938 · 12 contributors · 45 comments
  • Followers: 16

Summary

A new view unpublished block content permission has been added to the block_content module. Previously, viewing an unpublished block (for example a Draft block created through Content Moderation) required either administer block content or access block library, both of which grant much broader access than simply viewing. Editors who had only the create and edit custom blocks permission could place unpublished blocks via Layout Builder but could not view them on the actual page. The new permission allows sites to grant scoped view access to unpublished block content without elevating those users to library or admin roles.

Impact

Affects site owners.

  • Permission change: New 'view unpublished block content' permission allows viewing draft block content without requiring 'administer block content' or 'access block library'.

Technical details

The permission is declared in block_content.permissions.yml. The access logic in BlockContentAccessControlHandler::checkAccess() was updated so that the view operation allows access when the entity is published, or when the account has either access block library OR the new view unpublished block content permission (evaluated with an OR combinator via allowedIfHasPermissions()). A addCacheableDependency($entity) call was also added to the view access result so that the published/unpublished state of the entity is correctly reflected in the cache metadata. Tests were updated to cover the new permission case and to correct an existing test-case label (view:unpublished:reusable:admin renamed to view:unpublished:reusable:library). Expected unauthorized access messages in REST/JSON:API tests now reflect the two-permission alternative: 'access block library' OR 'view unpublished block content'.

Upgrade

No code changes are required. Sites using Content Moderation with block content should review editor roles and consider granting the new view unpublished block content permission instead of the broader access block library or administer block content permissions if the only need is viewing draft blocks.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: December 17, 2018
  • Committed: June 3, 2026 (7 years and 3 commits later)

Key contributors

  • jurgenr
  • acbramley (PreviousNext)
  • smustgrave (Mobomo)
  • pameeela (Technocrat)
  • gábor hojtsy (Acquia)

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3486503: Add a file parsing cache collector to replace some uses of FileCache
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Core
  • Type: Normal task
  • Version: 11.4.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 14 files, +344/−121 · php +327/−119 · yml +17/−2
  • Diff: 0be9248
  • Discussion: #3486503 · 7 contributors · 96 comments
  • Followers: 24

Summary

A new database-backed cache bin, cache.file_parsing, is introduced to store the results of YAML file parsing across cache clears. Unlike the existing FileCache (APCu), this bin is intentionally excluded from the cache-clear tag system, so its contents survive drush cr and the UI cache-clear form. Invalidation happens via file modification time checks instead. Library definition parsing (*.libraries.yml) and route YAML discovery now use this persistent cache, reducing redundant file reads and freeing APCu for higher-frequency data. Sites with multiple web heads or mixed CLI and web processes will benefit most, since APCu is process-local while the new bin is database-backed and shared.

Impact

Affects module maintainers.

  • Deprecation: LibraryDiscoveryParser::__construct() and YamlRouteDiscovery::__construct() require a new $yamlCacheCollector argument; omitting it is deprecated in drupal:11.4.0 and will be required in drupal:12.0.0.
  • New API: New FileParsingCacheCollectorBase, YamlCacheCollector, and YamlCacheCollectorDiscovery classes provide a reusable, persistent file-parse caching layer backed by the new cache.file_parsing bin.

Technical details

The fix introduces an abstract FileParsingCacheCollectorBase class in Drupal\Core\Utility, extending CacheCollector. Each cache item stores an array keyed by absolute file path, where each entry holds the file's mtime and the parsed result. On ::get($key) the collector loads the whole item lazily with one cache read, then validates filemtime() per file before returning the stored data; a mtime mismatch falls through to ::resolveCacheMiss() which re-parses the file. The ::updateCache() override adds garbage collection: any file entry whose stored mtime is older than 90 days and whose file no longer exists is removed before the parent writes back. CacheCollector::destruct() now also calls $this->reset() after updateCache() to release the in-memory storage.

YamlCacheCollector (in Drupal\Core\Utility) is the concrete subclass that implements ::parseFile() via Yaml::decode(). YamlCacheCollectorDiscovery (in Drupal\Core\Discovery) subclasses YamlDiscovery and accepts a CacheCollectorInterface; it overrides ::findAll() and ::decode() to delegate file reads to the collector, then calls ::destruct() when discovery finishes to release memory immediately.

Two services are wired in core.services.yml: library.parsing_cache (a YamlCacheCollector backed by cache.file_parsing, tagged needs_destruction) feeds into library.discovery.parser, and router.yaml_cache_collector feeds into router.builder.yaml which now uses YamlCacheCollectorDiscovery instead of bare YamlDiscovery. The cache.file_parsing bin is defined without the cache.bin service tag so standard cache invalidation logic skips it entirely.

LibraryDiscoveryParser::__construct() gains a new optional ?YamlCacheCollector $yamlCacheCollector argument and also converts its property declarations to constructor promotion. YamlRouteDiscovery::__construct() gains an optional ?CacheCollectorInterface $yamlCacheCollector argument. Both emit an E_USER_DEPRECATED if the argument is omitted, noting the argument will be required in 12.0.0. The second commit (5768a3d2) moves YamlCacheCollectorTest from Drupal\Tests\Core\Cache to Drupal\Tests\Core\Utility to match the class location.

Upgrade

If you directly instantiate LibraryDiscoveryParser or YamlRouteDiscovery, pass a YamlCacheCollector as the final constructor argument to avoid the deprecation warning:

use Drupal\Core\Utility\YamlCacheCollector;

// For library discovery:
$yaml_cache_collector = new YamlCacheCollector(
  'library.parsing_cache',
  $cache_file_parsing_backend, // the cache.file_parsing service
  $lock,
  $time,
);
$parser = new LibraryDiscoveryParser(
  $root, $module_handler, $theme_manager, $stream_wrapper_manager,
  $libraries_directory_file_finder, $extension_path_resolver,
  $component_plugin_manager,
  $yaml_cache_collector,  // new required argument in 12.0.0
);

// For route discovery:
$yaml_cache_collector = new YamlCacheCollector(
  'router.parsing_cache',
  $cache_file_parsing_backend,
  $lock,
  $time,
);
$discovery = new YamlRouteDiscovery(
  $module_handler,
  $controller_resolver,
  $yaml_cache_collector,  // new required argument in 12.0.0
);

If you inject these classes as services, Drupal's service container will supply the argument automatically once you update your service definition to match the new signature.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: November 9, 2024
  • Committed: June 3, 2026 (1 year and 4 commits later)

Key contributors

  • alexpott (Acro Commerce)
  • catch (Tag1 Consulting, Inc.)
  • nicxvan
  • daffie (Finalist)
  • berdir (MD Systems GmbH)
  • godotislate

Collaboration

During the first (abandoned) implementation attempt in late 2024, alexpott raised the concern about the InstallerExistingSettingsTest table-creation expectation that had been added to prevent cache pollution during early install phases. catch worked through the issue and concluded that the new bin's mtime-based invalidation makes it safe to create the table in that early installer state, unlike the regular cache backends the test was originally guarding against. alexpott and catch also discussed the database connection handling: after reviewing @daffie's concern about ::isActiveConnection() vs ::getConnectionInfo(), they agreed to use getConnectionInfo('default') to check availability and then getConnection('default', 'default') for explicit key and target, since the class can create database tables and there was evidence FileCache is called by run-tests.sh when rendering test results.

In the second (successful) attempt, berdir raised through a Slack thread that the per-file database approach added roughly 1700 database queries on a typical setup, and that for small YAML files the database lookups were slower than re-parsing. That observation was a key factor in the redesign: rather than one database query per file (the first MR's approach), the final implementation uses the CacheCollector pattern so all files for a given discovery type are stored in a single cache item, resulting in one database read and one write per collector lifetime.

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3592887: AttributeRouteDiscovery does not cleanly handle invalid classes
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Core
  • Type: Normal bug report
  • Version: 11.4.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 5 files, +76/−5 · php +74/−5 · dist +2
  • Diff: b0d5f3a
  • Discussion: #3592887 · 4 contributors · 25 comments
  • Followers: 7

Summary

Sites running modules that include "compatibility shim" controllers, such as TFA 1.x on Drupal 11.4, were seeing a PHP fatal error during cache clear or drush cr. The attribute-based route discovery introduced in #3311365 scanned every PHP class file in a module and tried to autoload it. If a class had an incompatible method signature relative to its parent, PHP raised a fatal error that was not caught, bringing down the routing system. This fix makes the discovery process skip such classes gracefully instead of crashing.

Impact

No upgrade or configuration changes required.

Technical details

Two changes land in AttributeRouteDiscovery. First, a fast pre-filter: before autoloading a class, the file's raw contents are read and checked with str_contains() for the strings #[Route or Routing\Attribute\Route. Files that match neither string are skipped entirely, avoiding the autoloader for the vast majority of classes. The approach has acknowledged edge cases (a comment containing #[Route is a false positive) but these are an accepted tradeoff since route discovery results are cached. Second, the class_exists() call inside createRouteCollection() is wrapped in a try/catch (\Error) block so that classes which do pass the string filter but still fail to autoload (e.g., a class extending a parent from a missing module) are silently skipped instead of propagating a fatal error. The catch targets \Error specifically; the commit comment notes that missing traits only became catchable as \Error in PHP 8.5, so the test fixture for this case uses a missing parent class instead, which is catchable on all supported PHP versions. Two intentionally invalid test fixtures are added to router_test_directory: TestInvalidController (an abstract method on a non-abstract class) and TestMissingDependency (a #[Route]-annotated class that extends a class from a non-existent module). Both are excluded from PHPStan. The broader challenge of centralizing fatal-error handling across all attribute discovery subsystems is tracked separately in #3586328.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: May 30, 2026
  • First commit: June 2, 2026 (2 days later)
  • Last commit: June 3, 2026 (1 day and 3 commits later)

Key contributors

  • cmlara (DM13 Security LLC)
  • longwave (Full Fat Things)
  • godotislate
  • catch (Tag1 Consulting, Inc.)

Collaboration

longwave proposed the str_contains() pre-filter approach after a Slack discussion with godotislate and catch (noted by godotislate in comment #16602879), then implemented it in MR !15945. cmlara, the issue reporter, tested the MR against TFA's codebase and confirmed the fix resolved the crash, while also flagging the false-positive risk of the string-matching approach and noting that \Throwable catches the load failure on PHP 8.4.

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3589626: clickLink in kernel tests erroneously prefixes some paths with /
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Core
  • Type: Normal bug report
  • Version: 11.4.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 1 file, +7/−4 · php +7/−4
  • Diff: b921ffe
  • Discussion: #3589626 · 5 contributors · 18 comments
  • Followers: 6

Summary

A bug in HttpKernelUiHelperTrait::drupalGet() caused clickLink() in kernel tests to navigate to the wrong URL when the clicked link's href was a bare query string (for example ?page=1). The old code prepended / to any path not starting with /, turning ?page=1 into /?page=1 and sending the test to the front page instead of the intended page. The fix teaches drupalGet() to leave query-string paths alone so they resolve relative to the current page URL as expected.

Impact

No upgrade or configuration changes required.

Technical details

In HttpKernelUiHelperTrait::drupalGet(), the guard that adds a leading / to bare relative paths now also checks whether the path starts with ?. The original condition !str_starts_with($path, '/') was expanded to !str_starts_with($path, '?') && !str_starts_with($path, '/'), so paths like ?page=1 pass through unchanged and are resolved relative to the current page URL by the underlying Symfony browser client. The @param docblock for $path was updated to document this distinction. This trait was introduced in #3582769 and getUrl() was added in #3583594.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: May 11, 2026
  • Committed: June 3, 2026 (22 days and 3 commits later)

Key contributors

  • mstrelan
  • joachim (Chicken)
  • dww (3281d Consulting)
  • catch (Tag1 Consulting, Inc.)

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #1499532: Respect explicitly set node path alias langcode on save
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Core
  • Type: Normal bug report
  • Version: 11.4.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 3 files, +49/−5 · php +49/−5
  • Diff: f30d64c
  • Discussion: #1499532 · 10 contributors · 39 comments
  • Followers: 16

Summary

On multilingual sites, setting a path alias to a specific language (or to language-neutral, LanguageInterface::LANGCODE_NOT_SPECIFIED) programmatically was silently ignored when saving a node. The alias was always stored with the node translation's language instead. A langcode-only update (same alias text, different language) was also dropped entirely. Both cases are now fixed: the explicitly provided alias langcode is respected on both create and update.

Impact

No upgrade or configuration changes required.

Technical details

Two bugs lived in PathItem::postSave() inside core/modules/path/src/Plugin/Field/FieldType/PathItem.php.

First, the langcode selection used the condition ($langcode && $pid) ? $langcode : $this->getLangcode(). Because $pid is absent when creating a new alias, any explicit langcode supplied at node-creation time was always discarded in favour of the entity's own langcode. The fix simplifies this to $langcode ?: $this->getLangcode(), so any non-empty explicit langcode wins for both new and existing aliases.

Second, the update branch only called $path_alias->save() when the alias text changed ($alias != $path_alias->getAlias()). A change to the langcode alone was never persisted. The fix introduces a $path_alias_changed flag that is set when either the alias text or the langcode ($alias_langcode !== $path_alias->language()->getId()) differs from the stored value, and save is triggered when either is true.

A third, related fix is in PathDefaultWidget::formElement(). The hidden langcode form field previously always forwarded $items[$delta]->langcode, which for unsaved items resolved to the entity's langcode and would have overridden any programmatic langcode set before the form was rendered. It now passes NULL when no $pid exists yet (new alias), allowing the save logic to apply the explicitly set value instead.

New kernel test cases in PathItemTest::testPathItem() cover: setting langcode to LANGCODE_NOT_SPECIFIED on a saved node's path field and re-saving (verifies the language-neutral alias is stored and the language-specific alias is gone), and creating a new node with LANGCODE_NOT_SPECIFIED in the path data (verifies the alias is created language-neutral from the start).

Upgrade

No API changes. If your code was previously working around this bug by manually editing the path alias after node save, that workaround can be removed. For example, code like:

// Before: workaround needed because langcode was ignored on save
$node->path->langcode = LanguageInterface::LANGCODE_NOT_SPECIFIED;
$node->save();
// Then manually fix the stored alias…
$alias = $alias_storage->load($pid);
$alias->set('langcode', LanguageInterface::LANGCODE_NOT_SPECIFIED);
$alias->save();

can now be simplified to:

// After: langcode is respected on save
$node->path->langcode = LanguageInterface::LANGCODE_NOT_SPECIFIED;
$node->save();

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: March 25, 2012
  • Committed: June 2, 2026 (14 years and 3 commits later)

Key contributors

  • q0rban
  • smustgrave (Mobomo)
  • joelpittet (The University of British Columbia)

Collaboration

joelpittet picked up this 13-year-old issue after smustgrave flagged it as a daily BSI target, noting that the original patch referenced $node->path['language'], which had long since become $node->path->langcode in modern Drupal. joelpittet opened MR !15411, rewrote the issue summary to the standard template, and disclosed in the thread that they used AI as an assistive tool while remaining the human reviewer: they questioned the extra $path_alias_changed flag and ran the tests both with and without the fix to confirm the red/green results before submitting. smustgrave independently verified the fix by running a drush php:eval snippet against a fresh install with two languages and confirmed the alias langcode changed from en to und with the patch applied.

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3549601: Allow --directory and @group to work together in run-tests.sh
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Core
  • Type: Normal feature request
  • Version: 11.4.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 5 files, +103/−19 · php +64/−14 · sh +39/−5
  • Diff: db775a3
  • Discussion: #3549601 · 6 contributors · 65 comments
  • Followers: 7

Summary

When running core/scripts/run-tests.sh with both the --directory option and a positional @group argument, the group filter was previously ignored and all tests in the directory were run. This made it impossible for contrib module maintainers to do the two things at once: limit test discovery to their module's directory (which avoids fatal errors from unrelated third-party modules) and still run only a specific test group. The fix makes the two options work together: --directory narrows the file scan and the group argument then filters the discovered tests down to the requested subset. The script now also prints a "Test discovery" header summarizing which directory, module, test suite(s), and group(s) are active for the run.

Impact

No upgrade or configuration changes required.

Technical details

The root cause was that in Drupal 11.2+ the run-tests.sh script adopted PHPUnit 11's test discovery API (PhpUnitTestDiscovery). When --directory was in use, getTestClasses() called the getTestListLimitedToDirectory() code path, but the positional group argument from Config::getTests() was never forwarded to the discovery layer; it was only used in the separate "groups-only" code path. The fix adds a $testGroups parameter to the public PhpUnitTestDiscovery::getTestClasses() and findAllClassFiles() methods, and to the private helpers getTestList() and getTestListLimitedToDirectory(). Both helpers now apply an array_intersect check against $item['groups'] and skip any test class whose groups do not overlap with the requested list. The call sites in run-tests.sh (simpletest_script_get_test_list() and the --list-files-json path) are updated to pass Config::getTests() as $testGroups. A new guard in Config::createFromCommandLine() throws a RuntimeException when --directory is combined with --class or --file, since those options are all file-based and mutually exclusive. This fix was built on top of the Symfony Console-based argument parsing introduced in #3536964.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: September 30, 2025
  • Committed: June 2, 2026 (8 months and 3 commits later)

Key contributors

  • jonathan1055
  • mondrake
  • godotislate
  • catch (Tag1 Consulting, Inc.)

Collaboration

The initial implementation by jonathan1055 kept the group-filtering logic inside run-tests.sh. mondrake reviewed and proposed moving the filter into PhpUnitTestDiscovery itself, arguing it made the logic more testable and fit the ongoing effort to strip complexity out of run-tests.sh. That restructuring landed as a separate commit on the same MR. After a subsequent rebase dropped part of the implementation, jonathan1055 noticed by testing against a real contrib pipeline (the webform module) that group filtering had silently stopped working again. mondrake identified the dropped logic, fixed it, and added kernel tests that verify that --directory plus a group argument returns strictly fewer results than --directory alone. godotislate independently pulled the branch and ran the full combination of options locally, posting detailed output to confirm correctness before the final commit.

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3587564: Move search functionality from node to Search module
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Core
  • Type: Normal task
  • Version: 11.4.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 49 files, +1124/−992 · php +1103/−978 · yml +21/−14
  • Diff: bcb6694
  • Discussion: #3587564 · 3 contributors · 18 comments
  • Followers: 6

Summary

A new search_node core submodule now owns all of the Search module integration that was previously bundled inside the Node module. Sites that use the built-in content search (the node_search search page) will need this new module enabled. An update hook handles the transition automatically on existing sites. This work is part of the broader effort to deprecate the Search module by extracting its integrations into dedicated submodules.

Impact

Affects module maintainers.

  • Deprecation: \Drupal\node\Plugin\Search\NodeSearch deprecated in drupal:11.4.0, removed in drupal:12.0.0; use \Drupal\search_node\Plugin\Search\SearchNode instead.
  • New API: New search_node core submodule introduced, owning the node_search plugin, its hooks, config, and Views data integration.

Technical details

The canonical node_search plugin class has moved from core/modules/node/src/Plugin/Search/NodeSearch.php (\Drupal\node\Plugin\Search\NodeSearch) to core/modules/search/modules/search_node/src/Plugin/Search/SearchNode.php (\Drupal\search_node\Plugin\Search\SearchNode). The old class is retained as a deprecated shell that simply extends the new one and fires E_USER_DEPRECATED in its constructor; the #[Search(id: 'node_search')] attribute now lives on SearchNode.

Several hooks previously implemented inside the Node module have been relocated. NodeSearchHooks (cron rankings aggregation) was re-namespaced into Drupal\search_node\Hook. A new SearchNodeHooks class implements hook_entity_predelete (clearing the search index when a node is deleted), hook_node_update (clearing removed translations from the index), hook_entity_view_display_alter (hiding field labels in the search_index view mode), and a hook_form_node_preview_form_select_alter to unset the search_index view mode option. The Views data for node_search_index, node_search_total, and node_search_dataset was extracted from NodeViewsData::getViewsData() into a new SearchNodeViewsHooks::viewsDataAlter() hook.

On the configuration side, the search.page.node_search.yml optional config file now declares search_node (not node) as its required module; the schema entry for search.plugin.node_search moved from node.schema.yml to a new search_node.schema.yml; and the core.entity_view_mode.node.search_index and core.entity_view_mode.node.search_result install configs moved from the Node module to search_node. The Node entity class itself was cleaned up: the preDelete() static method and the translation-removal call in postSave() were deleted, since those responsibilities now live in SearchNodeHooks.

Upgrade

Code that subclasses or type-hints \Drupal\node\Plugin\Search\NodeSearch must be updated:

// Before (deprecated in drupal:11.4.0, removed in drupal:12.0.0):
use Drupal\node\Plugin\Search\NodeSearch;

// After:
use Drupal\search_node\Plugin\Search\SearchNode;

The search_node module must be enabled wherever the node_search search page is in use. On existing sites an update hook handles this automatically, but custom install profiles or test environments that enable modules explicitly should add search_node to their module list.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: April 29, 2026
  • First commit: May 26, 2026 (27 days later)
  • Last commit: June 2, 2026 (7 days and 3 commits later)

Key contributors

  • quietone (PreviousNext)
  • smustgrave (Mobomo)
  • berdir (MD Systems GmbH)
  • catch (Tag1 Consulting, Inc.)

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #2875033: Optimize joins and table selection in SQL entity query implementation
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Core
  • Type: Normal task
  • Version: 11.4.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 9 files, +205/−34 · php +205/−34
  • Diff: 702159f
  • Discussion: #2875033 · 36 contributors · 119 comments
  • Followers: 87

Summary

The SQL entity query layer now generates significantly fewer joins and avoids unnecessary GROUP BY clauses in many common scenarios. Previously, querying a base-table property like uuid would join the table to itself; querying two fields from the data table would add two independent joins to the same table; following an entity reference with multiple conditions would repeat the reference join for each condition; and any entity type with a data table would unconditionally mark the query as non-simple, adding a GROUP BY even when only one row per entity was possible. All of these redundancies are fixed. Sites with large datasets or many translations should see measurable query-time improvements for entity queries, including those driven by JSON:API and Views.

Impact

Affects module maintainers.

  • API change: ConditionInterface requires two new methods setQuery() and getQuery(); Query::getTables() no longer accepts a $sql_query argument (deprecated drupal:11.4.0, removed drupal:13.0.0).
  • Deprecation: Query::getTables($sql_query) with an argument and passing a Condition from a different query into condition() are both deprecated in drupal:11.4.0 and removed in drupal:13.0.0.
  • New API: LanguageInterface::LANGCODE_DEFAULT is now a valid langcode argument to entity-query condition() calls; it joins data tables on default_langcode = 1 rather than a literal language match.

Technical details

The root cause was that Query::getTables() created a fresh Tables instance on every call, so each nested Condition (inside AND/OR groups) got its own join-tracking state and could not deduplicate against joins already added by sibling conditions. The fix introduces doGetTables(), which stores and reuses a single Tables instance per query. All Condition objects that belong to the same query now share one Tables instance, so the entityTables and fieldTables arrays correctly prevent the same join from being added twice.

Five specific join-redundancy bugs are fixed by this shared state:

  1. Base-table self-join: prepare() stores the base table name in query metadata under entity_query_base_table. The Tables constructor pre-populates entityTables with that entry, so a condition on a base-table column (e.g., uuid) resolves directly to base_table instead of joining the table to itself.

  2. Data-table joins: ensureEntityTable() now keys entries by $index_prefix . $base_table . '.' . $table . ($langcode ? '.' . $langcode : ''), properly deduplicating joins regardless of which condition requests them. The old key was incorrect when the base table differed from 'base_table'.

  3. Entity-reference joins: addNextBaseTable() now caches its result in a nextBaseTables array keyed by $table.$sql_column, so following the same reference path in two conditions produces one join instead of two.

  4. simple_query / GROUP BY elimination: prepare() now always starts with simple_query = TRUE. Inside Tables::addField(), the flag is only set to FALSE when the data-table join has no langcode restriction (a langcode-restricted join is 1-to-1, so no fan-out occurs). Queries with a specific language condition or with a single-cardinality field no longer add GROUP BY.

  5. Multi-value AND conditions: A new tableIndexPrefix property on Condition ensures that each top-level andConditionGroup() wrapping a multi-value field gets its own index namespace. This keeps the well-documented behaviour (placing ->andConditionGroup()->condition('field', 'X') and ->andConditionGroup()->condition('field', 'Y') as siblings to find entities that have both values) while still deduplicating joins within a single group.

Additionally, LanguageInterface::LANGCODE_DEFAULT is now recognised in addJoin(). When it is passed for a data table or revision data table, the join condition uses default_langcode = 1 rather than a literal language-code match, allowing callers to query against the entity's default language without knowing its code.

Two deprecations ship with this change: passing $sql_query to Query::getTables() is deprecated in drupal:11.4.0 and removed in drupal:13.0.0 (use doGetTables() or, for custom subclasses, override doGetTables()); passing a Condition created by a different query instance to another query's condition() is also deprecated in drupal:11.4.0 and removed in drupal:13.0.0.

ConditionInterface gains two new required methods, setQuery(QueryInterface $query) and getQuery(), which the base ConditionBase class implements by propagating the query reference recursively through nested conditions.

Upgrade

Implementations of \Drupal\Core\Entity\Query\ConditionInterface must add two new methods:

public function setQuery(QueryInterface $query): static {
    $this->query = $query;
    foreach ($this->conditions as $condition) {
        if ($condition['field'] instanceof ConditionInterface) {
            $condition['field']->setQuery($query);
        }
    }
    return $this;
}

public function getQuery(): QueryInterface {
    return $this->query;
}

If your code calls getTables() with a $sql_query argument, remove it:

// Before (deprecated):
$tables = $this->query->getTables($this->sqlQuery);

// After:
$tables = $this->query->doGetTables();

If your code reuses a condition group from one query inside a different query, stop doing so:

// Deprecated pattern – $query1 and $query2 are separate instances:
$group = $query1->andConditionGroup()->condition('field', 'value');
$query2->condition($group);

// Correct – always create condition groups from the query you are building:
$group = $query2->andConditionGroup()->condition('field', 'value');
$query2->condition($group);

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: May 2, 2017
  • Committed: June 2, 2026 (9 years and 3 commits later)

Key contributors

  • catch (Tag1 Consulting, Inc.)
  • driskell (Other Media)
  • berdir (MD Systems GmbH)
  • amateescu
  • kaythay (Aten Design Group)
  • joachim
  • rlmumford
  • heddn (MTech, LLC)
  • ghost of drupal past
  • alexbrut (Smile)
  • etroid
  • andreyks (Adyax)
  • cilefen
  • damondt
  • davidwhthomas
  • itsekhmistro (Adyax)
  • mstef
  • alexpott (Acro Commerce)

Collaboration

driskell drove the final implementation that landed. After years of stalled patches, they rewrote the approach from first principles on 11.x: introduced the shared Tables singleton, tableIndexPrefix for multi-value AND semantics, doGetTables(), LANGCODE_DEFAULT join support, and the setQuery()/getQuery() API. In comment #71 they surfaced the obscure andConditionGroup cross-delta behaviour as a design question and clearly documented why preserving it required the prefix approach, then in comment #74 they committed to a follow-up to clean it up rather than let it block the fix. joachim provided the framing in comment #75 that confirmed this was a refactoring issue and that the andConditionGroup question should stay separate.

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.
